meta.description: Evaluates clinical utility of a binary prediction model from a single clinical CSV by fitting a logistic decision-curve model with rmda, plotting decision and clinical-impact curves, and exporting DCA model object, summary text, and PDFs.

scientific_integrity.detail: No fabrication of statistical values; DCA outputs derive directly from rmda computations on user-provided data

practice_boundaries.detail: No medical diagnoses; skill evaluates model clinical utility across thresholds without prescriptive conclusions

methodological_ground.detail: Logistic decision-curve analysis with Vickers-Elkin net benefit is established methodology; cohort/case-control distinction correctly implemented

code_usability.detail: Syntactically correct; rmda::decision_curve() called with validated arguments; tryCatch wraps model fitting; clean exit codes; no infinite loops

functional_suitability.note: Covers binary prediction DCA with cohort/case-control designs, NB/sNB output, confidence intervals, and full plot customization; minor gap: 20-row minimum not surfaced in SKILL.md Input Format

reliability.note: Six SKILL_* codes cover all major failure modes; model fitting wrapped in tryCatch; package messages captured and logged; one gap: no explicit behavior documented when rmda model fitting produces degenerate thresholds

performance_context.note: SKILL.md is 293 lines; references directory for algorithm details; compute warning for fine threshold grids documented; one minor gap: no progressive disclosure hint for large datasets

agent_usability.note: Agent Response Contract specifies 5 structured output fields; When to Read External Files table guides navigation; option group separation in main.R improves clarity; one gap: timeout guidance for fine threshold grids missing

human_usability.note: Natural trigger language; When Not to Use well-structured with four explicit exclusions; character outcome documentation present; one gap: recode guidance for character outcomes not provided

security.note: No hardcoded credentials; no eval/exec; formula built via sprintf with backtick-quoted column names preventing injection; one minor gap: file path not checked for traversal

maintainability.note: Clean 4-script modular structure; option groups separated into create_core_option_list() and create_plot_option_list(); implementation checklist present; no unit tests in polished package

agent_specific.note: Precise trigger language targeting DCA specifically; SKILL.md under 500 lines; clean RDS+PDF+TXT output interface; idempotent via set.seed; escape hatches present; composability slightly limited by single-file-only input design

inputs.label: Standard case-control DCA with fustat and riskScore
inputs.note: Full workflow documented; binary coercion handled; Agent Response Contract complete

assertions.note: When Not to Use and Input Validation sections both present

inputs.label: Character-encoded outcome column instead of 0/1
inputs.note: Character outcome correctly fails with SKILL_INVALID_PARAMETER; recode guidance absent

assertions.note: as.numeric() coercion produces NA which fails the {0,1} check

assertions.text: Factor/character outcome behavior documented in SKILL.md
assertions.note: Input Format Requirements notes factor/character -> SKILL_INVALID_PARAMETER

assertions.text: Guidance on how to recode character outcomes provided
assertions.note: No recode example or fix suggestion in SKILL.md; user must know to use ifelse() or recode independently

assertions.text: Logical TRUE/FALSE coercion handled and documented
assertions.note: Input Format notes TRUE->1, FALSE->0 conversion

key_strengths: Precise task scoping: description and When Not to Use section cleanly exclude survival, ROC, nomogram, and multiclass requests
Robust input validation: binary outcome coercion, class balance checks, and threshold grid validation all correctly implemented
Plot customization fully exposed as CLI parameters, enabling reproducible publication-quality figures
withCallingHandlers pattern captures and logs rmda package messages without crashing the pipeline
Agent Response Contract provides structured 5-point summary for downstream automation

recommendations.title: Surface 20-row minimum in SKILL.md Input Format section
recommendations.problem: The 20-row minimum and class balance requirements (5 positive, 5 negative) are enforced in code but not listed in the SKILL.md Input Format Requirements section, so users discover them only at runtime.
recommendations.root_cause: Input Format Requirements section describes structural requirements (file extension, row names, column types) but omits statistical minimum sample size thresholds.
recommendations.fix: Add to Input Format Requirements: 'At least 20 rows, 5 positive outcomes (outcome=1), and 5 negative outcomes (outcome=0) are required for DCA to produce meaningful results.'

recommendations.title: Add recode guidance for character-encoded outcomes
recommendations.problem: When factor or character outcome values are provided, the error only states that 0/1 encoding is required without showing how to convert common character encodings.
recommendations.root_cause: Error Handling table lists the error code but does not include a fix example for the most common case (character recoding).
recommendations.fix: Add a note to the SKILL_INVALID_PARAMETER row or Input Format section: 'To recode character outcomes, preprocess with: df$outcome <- ifelse(df$outcome == "positive", 1, 0)'

recommendations.title: Add timeout guidance for fine threshold grids
recommendations.problem: The Arguments table warns that threshold_by < 0.005 significantly increases computation time, but provides no guidance on appropriate --timeout_seconds values.
recommendations.root_cause: The computation warning was added to the argument description but was not linked to timeout parameter guidance.
recommendations.fix: Add a note to the threshold_by row: 'For threshold_by < 0.005 on datasets > 1000 rows, consider --timeout_seconds 300 or higher.'
